mn152:0.2: Sutta The Development of the Faculties
mn152:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Kajjaṅgalas in the Bamboo Forest.
mn152:2.1: Then the young brahman Uttara, a student of Pārāsiri [Pārāsivi] went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged friendly greetings & courtesies.
mn152:2.2: After this exchange of courteous greetings he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, the Blessed One said to him:
mn152:2.3: “Uttara, does the brahman Pārāsiri teach his followers the development of the faculties?”
mn152:2.5: “And how does he teach his followers the development of the faculties?”
mn152:2.6: “There is the case where one does not see forms with the eye, or hear sounds with the ear [in a trance of non-perception].
mn152:2.7: That’s how the brahman Pārāsiri teaches his followers the development of the faculties.”
mn152:2.8: “That being the case, Uttara, then a blind person will have developed faculties, and a deaf person will have developed faculties,
mn152:2.9: according to the words of the brahman Pārāsiri.
mn152:2.10: For a blind person does not see forms with the eye, and a deaf person does not hear sounds with the ear.”
mn152:2.11: When this was said, the young brahman Uttara sat silent & abashed, his shoulders slumped, his head down, brooding, at a loss for words.
mn152:3.1: The Blessed One—noticing that Uttara was sitting silent & abashed, his shoulders slumped, his head down, brooding, at a loss for words—said to Ven. Ānanda,
mn152:3.2: “Ānanda, the development of the faculties that the brahman Pārāsiri teaches his followers is one thing, but the unexcelled development of the faculties in the discipline of a noble one is something else entirely.”
mn152:3.3: “Now is the time, O Blessed One.
mn152:3.4: Now is the time, O One Well-Gone, for the Blessed One to teach the unexcelled development of the faculties in the discipline of the noble one. Having heard the Blessed One, the monks will remember it.”
mn152:3.5: “In that case, Ānanda, listen & pay close attention. I will speak.”
mn152:3.6: “As you say, lord,” Ven. Ānanda responded to the Blessed One.
mn152:4.1: “Now how, Ānanda, in the discipline of a noble one is there the unexcelled development of the faculties?
mn152:4.2: There is the case where, when seeing a form with the eye, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:4.4: ‘This agreeable (reaction) has arisen in me, this disagreeable (reaction)… this agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) has arisen in me.
mn152:4.5: And that is fabricated, gross, dependently co-arisen.
mn152:4.6: But this is peaceful, this is exquisite, i.e.,
mn152:4.8: With that, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases,
mn152:4.10: Just as a man with good eyes, having closed them, might open them; or having opened them, might close them,
mn152:4.11: that is how quickly, how rapidly, how easily, no matter what it refers to, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases, and equanimity takes a stance.
mn152:4.12: In the discipline of a noble one, this is called the unexcelled development of the faculties with regard to forms cognizable by the eye.
mn152:5.1: “And further, when hearing a sound with the ear, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:5.3: ‘This agreeable (reaction) has arisen in me, this disagreeable (reaction)… this agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) has arisen in me.
mn152:5.4: And that is fabricated, gross, dependently co-arisen.
mn152:5.5: But this is peaceful, this is exquisite, i.e.,
mn152:5.7: With that, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases,
mn152:5.9: Just as a strong man might easily snap his fingers, that is how quickly, how rapidly, how easily, no matter what it refers to, the arisen agreeable…
mn152:5.10: disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases, and equanimity takes a stance.
mn152:5.11: In the discipline of a noble one, this is called the unexcelled development of the faculties with regard to sounds cognizable by the ear.
mn152:6.1: “And further, when smelling an aroma with the nose, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:6.3: ‘This agreeable (reaction) has arisen in me, this disagreeable (reaction)… this agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) has arisen in me.
mn152:6.4: And that is fabricated, gross, dependently co-arisen.
mn152:6.5: But this is peaceful, this is exquisite, i.e.,
mn152:6.7: With that, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases,
mn152:6.9: Just as drops of water roll off a gently sloping lotus leaf & do not remain there,
mn152:6.10: that is how quickly, how rapidly, how easily, no matter what it refers to, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases, and equanimity takes a stance.
mn152:6.11: In the discipline of a noble one, this is called the unexcelled development of the faculties with regard to aromas cognizable by the nose.
mn152:7.1: “And further, when tasting a flavor with the tongue, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:7.3: ‘This agreeable (reaction) has arisen in me, this disagreeable (reaction)… this agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) has arisen in me.
mn152:7.4: And that is fabricated, gross, dependently co-arisen.
mn152:7.5: But this is peaceful, this is exquisite, i.e.,
mn152:7.7: With that, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases,
mn152:7.9: Just as a strong man might easily spit out a ball of saliva gathered on the tip of his tongue,
mn152:7.10: that is how quickly, how rapidly, how easily, no matter what it refers to, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases, and equanimity takes a stance.
mn152:7.11: In the discipline of a noble one, this is called the unexcelled development of the faculties with regard to flavors cognizable by the tongue.
mn152:8.1: “And further, when touching a tactile sensation with the body, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:8.3: ‘This agreeable (reaction) has arisen in me, this disagreeable (reaction)… this agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) has arisen in me.
mn152:8.4: And that is fabricated, gross, dependently co-arisen.
mn152:8.5: But this is peaceful, this is exquisite, i.e.,
mn152:8.7: With that, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases,
mn152:8.9: Just as a strong man might easily extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm,
mn152:8.10: that is how quickly, how rapidly, how easily, no matter what it refers to, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases, and equanimity takes a stance.
mn152:8.11: In the discipline of a noble one, this is called the unexcelled development of the faculties with regard to tactile sensations cognizable by the body.
mn152:9.1: “And further, when cognizing an idea with the intellect, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:9.3: ‘This agreeable (reaction) has arisen in me, this disagreeable (reaction)… this agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) has arisen in me.
mn152:9.4: And that is fabricated, gross, dependently co-arisen.
mn152:9.5: But this is peaceful, this is exquisite, i.e.,
mn152:9.7: With that, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases,
mn152:9.9: Just as a strong man might let two or three drops of water fall onto an iron pan heated all day:
mn152:9.10: Slow would the falling of the drops of water, but they quickly would vanish & disappear.
mn152:9.11: That is how quickly, how rapidly, how easily, no matter what it refers to, the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction) ceases, and equanimity takes a stance.
mn152:9.12: In the discipline of a noble one, this is called the unexcelled development of the faculties with regard to ideas cognizable by the intellect.
mn152:10.1: “And how is one a person in training, someone following the way?
mn152:10.2: There is the case where, when seeing a form with the eye, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:10.3: He feels horrified, humiliated, & disgusted with the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:10.4: “When hearing a sound with the ear.…
mn152:10.5: When smelling an aroma with the nose.…
mn152:10.6: When tasting a flavor with the tongue.…
mn152:10.7: When touching a tactile sensation with the body.…
mn152:10.8: When cognizing an idea with the intellect, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:10.9: He feels horrified, humiliated, & disgusted with the arisen agreeable… disagreeable… agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:10.10: “This is how one is a person in training, someone following the way.
mn152:11-15.1: “And how is one a noble one with developed faculties?
mn152:11-15.2: There is the case where, when seeing a form with the eye, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:11-15.3: If he wants, he remains percipient of loathsomeness in the presence of what is not loathsome.
mn152:11-15.4: If he wants, he remains percipient of unloathsomeness in the presence of what is loathsome.
mn152:11-15.5: If he wants, he remains percipient of loathsomeness in the presence of what is not loathsome & what is.
mn152:11-15.6: If he wants, he remains percipient of unloathsomeness in the presence of what is loathsome & what is not.
mn152:11-15.7: If he wants—in the presence of what is loathsome & what is not—cutting himself off from both, he remains equanimous, alert, & mindful.
mn152:16.1: “When hearing a sound with the ear.…
mn152:16.2: When smelling an aroma with the nose.…
mn152:16.3: When tasting a flavor with the tongue.…
mn152:16.4: When touching a tactile sensation with the body.…
mn152:16.5: When cognizing an idea with the intellect, there arises in a monk an agreeable (reaction), a disagreeable (reaction), an agreeable & disagreeable (reaction).
mn152:16.6: If he wants, he remains percipient of loathsomeness in the presence of what is not loathsome.
mn152:16.7: If he wants, he remains percipient of unloathsomeness in the presence of what is loathsome.
mn152:16.8: If he wants, he remains percipient of loathsomeness in the presence of what is not loathsome & what is.
mn152:16.9: If he wants, he remains percipient of unloathsomeness in the presence of what is loathsome & what is not.
mn152:16.10: If he wants—in the presence of what is loathsome & what is not—cutting himself off from both, he remains equanimous, alert, & mindful.
mn152:16.11: “This is how one is a noble one with developed faculties.
mn152:17.1: “So, Ānanda, I have taught you the unexcelled development of the faculties in the discipline of a noble one; I have taught you how one is a person in training, someone following the way; I have taught you how one is a noble one with developed faculties.
mn152:18.1: Whatever a teacher should do—seeking the welfare of his disciples, out of sympathy for them—that have I done for you.
mn152:18.2: Over there are the roots of trees; over there, empty dwellings. Practice jhāna, Ānanda. Don’t be heedless. Don’t later fall into remorse. That is our message to you all.”
mn152:18.3: That is what the Blessed One said.
mn152:18.4: Gratified, Ven. Ānanda delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
